element_introduction_order.mastery_context: fundamentally determines gameplay style, spell availability, equipment restrictions, and story progression
oblique perspective mouse control with hotkeys; warrior-specific emphasis on armor and melee weapons
dynamically blacks out unseen portions of screen based on line of sight; requires spatial awareness and careful exploration
killing enemies and monsters to gain experience points; foundation for progression
gathering items, equipping gear, selling to traders; items wear down and require repair
Conjurer: Beast Scrolls and monster summoning/taming; Wizard: largest spell array, teleport, invisibility, healing; Warrior: recharging special abilities instead of mana
complex magic system inspired by Magic: The Gathering; allows strategic spell interaction
NPCs follow Jack to assist or require protection; cannot be controlled or equipped by player
player must collect three staff pieces (Halberd of Horrendous, Heart of Nox, Weirdling Beast) from faction-controlled locations
each faction guards one staff piece; representing non-chosen classes; leads to final assembly and confrontation
evil sorceress and necromancer army; requires assembled Staff of Oblivion to defeat; class-dependent unique ending

typical_relief_triggers: trader NPC interactions for item repair/sale
typical_tension_triggers: fog of war revealing hidden enemies
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: steady escalation with class-dependent progression rhythm; mix of exploratory grinding phases and story-driven combat encounters; TrueSight fog of war creates moment-to-moment tension through limited visibility

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: multiple dungeons and wilderness areas can be explored in non-linear order; optional beast scrolls and item discovery

difficulty_curve.early_game: learning curve focused on class mechanics and combat fundamentals; fog of war creates cautious exploration
difficulty_curve.mid_game: exponential increase through staff piece collection from enemy factions; complex spell combinations and NPC escort missions introduce tactical demands
difficulty_curve.late_game: faction confrontations escalate difficulty; final assembly and Hecubah encounter as climactic challenge
notable_spikes: transition to each faction-controlled location (Fire Knights Dün Mir, Castle Galava, Temple of Ix)
Hecubah and necromancer army final confrontation

notable_level_patterns.description: Multiple dungeons and wilderness areas where player kills enemies, collects experience and loot, and discovers items. Fog of war forces careful forward progression.
Player must penetrate enemy faction territory to retrieve staff pieces. Each stronghold represents a major difficulty spike and is guarded by faction-specific enemies and champions.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Dün Mir (Fire Knights), Castle Galava (Wizards), Temple of Ix (Conjurers)

notable_level_patterns.description: Temporary NPC allies follow Jack through levels; player must protect them while progressing, adding defensive responsibility to combat.
Warrior, Conjurer, and Wizard paths follow largely different linear storylines with unique endings, forcing three completely different progression experiences through similar spatial layouts.
